company.name: Salabarria Capital
company.product_name: Life Settlement Actuarial Valuation and Portfolio Analytics Platform
company.blurb: Internal institutional command center for a life settlement investment fund. The product helps actuarial analysts and portfolio managers value policies, model premium obligations, simulate mortality outcomes, compare IRR distributions, monitor actual-to-expected mortality, and create auditable valuation/reporting packages.
company.audience: Actuarial analysts, portfolio managers, investment committee users, compliance/reporting users, data engineers, and PII-privileged administrators.

product_principles.principle: Visual-first: every screen should show the decision before the table.
Institutional, not generic SaaS: dark financial command-center shell, dense metrics, strong hierarchy, no playful consumer styling.
Auditability is a UX feature: show assumption set, valuation run, random seed, ledger type, and engine version wherever outputs appear.
Privacy by design: insured PII must be segregated; analytics views use anonymized insured IDs only.
Mobile-friendly, but desktop-first for actual actuarial work. Mobile should support quick review, not full data entry.

brand_and_visual_language.tone: Quietly lethal institutional finance. Sharp, serious, premium, high-trust. Avoid cartoon fintech and template dashboard energy.

shape_language.card_style: Warm ivory panels, low-contrast borders, soft shadows, dark chart frames.
shape_language.density: High-density metrics with visual breathing room; fewer paragraphs, more decision objects.

example.name: Live Vercel prototype
example.description: Current visual dashboard prototype with demo/anonymized data only.

example.name: GitHub repository
example.description: Next.js prototype source code and design context.

example.name: Portfolio Dashboard
example.description: Fund-level cockpit showing policies, score, IRR, LE deviation, recommendation mix, and highest-opportunity/highest-risk callouts.

example.name: Policy Intelligence
example.description: Policy-level valuation memo, BUY/REVIEW/PASS score, IRR, MOIC, premium burden, NLG, contestability, and risk flags.

example.name: Underwriting Intelligence
example.description: Life expectancy challenge view showing actuary LE vs platform-adjusted LE, mortality drivers, data quality, and investment impact.

example.name: Simulation Engine
example.description: Monte Carlo / CTE view for IRR percentile fan, downside contributors, liquidity shortfall, and reproducible run metadata.

example.name: A/E Mortality Monitor
example.description: Actual-to-expected mortality dashboard with IBNR adjustment, exposure, expected deaths, actual deaths, and claims triangle.

example.name: Reporting Ledger
example.description: Compliance and export queue showing economic/statutory ledgers, audit events, assumption sets, PII isolation, and report package status.

information_architecture.screen: Policy valuation, premium schedule, survival curve, expected cash flows, audit history.
IRR distributions, CTE, cash flow projections, liquidity stress, cohort segmentation.
Expected vs actual deaths, claims triangles, vintage/duration/impairment segmentation.
Economic/statutory ledger separation, reserve reporting, AoVR-ready exports, audit approval.
Policy PDFs, premium schedules, LE reports, medical record packets, anonymization and validation pipeline.

technical_notes.note: Prototype is Next.js with static demo data; no real PII, no sensitive medical records, no production database.
Production target is Python for orchestration/API/reporting plus Rust for mortality curves, cash-flow projection, IRR, and Monte Carlo kernels.
Production storage should be PostgreSQL with temporal versioning, immutable valuation runs, assumption set history, premium schedule versions, and segregated PII domain.
Every production valuation must be reproducible from valuation_run_id, assumption_set_id, mortality table version, premium schedule version, input snapshot, random seed, and engine version.
Do not expose insured PII to simulation engines, LLMs, investor reports, or general analytics screens.
